events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 70,
  "value": 6,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "6-foot fall protection trigger height ignored — work performed at or above 6 ft without required fall protection measures in place",
    "Fall protection system absent or not engaged where OSHA trigger height is met or exceeded",
    "Installed fall protection components defective or non-compliant with Subpart M requirements"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately at all locations where the 6-ft trigger is exceeded without fall protection",
    "Install, inspect, and certify compliant fall arrest or fall restraint systems before resuming elevated work",
    "Conduct site-wide audit to identify all areas where trigger height was ignored and document corrective actions",
    "Retrain crews on OSHA 1926 Subpart M trigger height requirements and fall protection protocols"
  ],
  "rationale": "OSHA 1926 Subpart M establishes 6 feet as the trigger height at which fall protection must be provided and used in construction. The observed condition indicates this trigger was ignored, meaning workers are exposed to fall hazards without required protection. This is a critical, willful-level non-compliance that poses immediate life-safety risk. At rough-complete stage, the fall protection system is either missing, improperly installed, or bypassed — classified as installed-defective and non-compliant.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart M — 1926.501(b)(1) unprotected sides/edges 6 ft or above",
    "OSHA 1926.501(b)(2) leading edges 6 ft or above",
    "OSHA 1926.501(b)(3) hoist areas 6 ft or above",
    "OSHA 1910.140 — personal fall protection systems requirements"
  ]
}
